steps.title: Identify the shift between two minerals
steps.content: The sentence transitions from describing painite to describing trona, which have opposite light transmission properties. The blank must correctly mark where this shift occurs.

steps.title: Apply the sentence boundary rule
steps.content:
The correct answer correctly uses a period to complete the thought about painite's classification as transparent, then begins a fresh sentence: “Allowing only some light to pass through, trona is instead classified as translucent.” This marks a clear boundary between two independent, complete thoughts.
The other three either create grammatical errors, contradict the passage's description of painite transmitting all light, or fail to properly separate these two minerals with correct punctuation.
That is the correct answer.
